narrative:
ALONE AGAINST THE DARK - Point Black Lighthouse

    You arrive at the remote Point Black Lighthouse on the rocky Maine coast.
    Lieutenant Warner called you: the lighthouse keeper has vanished.
    But when you examine the logs, something is wrong. The keeper was dead for two weeks
    before anyone found him—yet someone has been writing in the logs ever since.

    The fog rolls in. The light blinks red. Something ancient stirs beneath the waves.
Player: Document the lighthouse with photographs

DM:  Marcus, armed with his camera and notebook, steps out onto the rocky coastline of Point Black Lighthouse. The fog clings to the air, obscuring the dark waters beneath. He takes a deep breath, focusing on capturing the eerie atmosphere in his photos.

[ROLL: investigate/normal]

As Marcus examines the lighthouse, he spots a few interesting details: the worn steps leading up to the door, the rusted metal railing, and the red warning lights flashing ominously. He snaps several photos, jotting down notes about the condition of the structure in his notebook.

With his documentation completed, Marcus starts to investigate further, heading towards the lighthouse itself. The fog swirls around him, making it difficult to see beyond a few feet.

[ITEM_FOUND: notebook]
Player: Interview local authorities about incident

DM:   As you approach the small coastal town, the fog begins to dissipate slightly, revealing the quaint buildings and harbor. You head towards the local police station, entering a dimly lit room where officers are huddled around maps and radio equipment.

[ROLL: persuade/normal]

You introduce yourself as an investigative journalist from Boston, explaining your interest in the recent events at Point Black Lighthouse. The officers exchange glances, clearly not accustomed to dealing with reporters. However, you manage to convince them to share what they know about the incident.

One officer hands you a thick folder containing reports and witness statements. Inside, you find accounts of strange occurrences around the lighthouse—creepy noises, flashing lights, and ghostly apparitions. Some people even claim to have seen dark shapes swimming in the waters nearby.

With new evidence in hand, you thank the officers for their help and head back out into the foggy town. The chilling wind picks up as you walk, carrying whispers of secrets long buried beneath the waves...

[ITEM_FOUND: folder]

=== PLAYER ACTION ===
Investigate local folklore regarding Point Black Lighthouse

You decide to explore the town's history and folklore, hoping to find clues about the strange events at Point Black Lighthouse. You visit the local library
Player: Search for documents and records

DM:  [ROLL: occult/hard]

As Marcus delves into the dusty archives of the town's library, he finds a collection of books on local folklore, ancient sea creatures, and maritime mysteries. With a sense of excitement, he begins to search through the volumes for any mention of Point Black Lighthouse or the eerie phenomena that have been plaguing it.

After hours of research, Marcus stumbles upon an old manuscript detailing the legends of the Deep Ones - amphibious creatures from the dark depths who are said to have once worshipped the Elder Gods in this region. According to the text, these beings still lurk beneath the waves, luring unsuspecting sailors to their doom with songs and illusions.

As Marcus reads further, he discovers that Point Black Lighthouse was built on an ancient burial ground of the Deep Ones. The manuscript warns that any who disturb their resting place will be cursed, suffering madness and death. Marcus shudders, feeling a chill run down his spine as he contemplates the implications of this discovery.

[SANITY_CHECK: 3]

Marcus returns to the lighthouse, his mind racing with thoughts of the Deep Ones and their dark powers. He can't help but feel that he has uncovered something truly terrible,

DM:  Marcus decides to search through the keeper's personal belongings for any clues about the strange happenings at Point Black Lighthouse. He heads back to the lighthouse, taking the worn wooden steps up to the top floor. The fog thickens around him as he enters the dimly lit quarters of the keeper.

[ROLL: investigate/normal]

Marcus carefully examines the keeper's belongings: a worn coat, a few personal items scattered across the table, and an old logbook tucked into a drawer. He opens the logbook, finding entries detailing his duties as well as strange occurrences—flashing lights, eerie noises, and feelings of being watched.

As Marcus reads further, he notices that the keeper's mood seemed to shift dramatically in the days leading up to his disappearance. The logs grew increasingly paranoid and agitated, with entries about strange dreams and feelings of being pursued by something malevolent. The last entry was dated just a few days ago:

"I can feel them closing in... they want me. I must finish the ritual."

Marcus shudders, feeling a cold chill run down his spine as he realizes that the keeper may have been trying to perform some dark ritual connected to the Deep Ones. He quickly snaps several photos of the logbook entries for evidence before gathering his things
Player: Uncover hidden truths through interviews

DM:  DM: As Marcus leaves the lighthouse, he decides to speak with local residents about their experiences and any information they might have regarding the strange happenings. He heads towards the town square, where a group of people are gathered around a fire, chatting and laughing.

[ROLL: persuade/normal]

Marcus approaches the group, introducing himself as an investigative journalist from Boston. He explains his interest in the events at Point Black Lighthouse and asks if anyone has experienced anything unusual or knows of any local legends that could provide insight.

The townsfolk exchange nervous glances, clearly uncomfortable with discussing such matters. However, one elderly woman steps forward, offering a chilling account of a time when her father was fishing near Point Black Lighthouse and witnessed something terrible:

"It was years ago now, but I'll never forget it. My father was out on his boat, just minding his own business, when suddenly the water around him began to boil and churn. A monstrous shape rose from the depths—something with long, writhing tentacles and glowing eyes that seemed to pierce through the darkness. My father couldn't move or scream; he was frozen in terror as the creature swallowed him whole."

The woman shivers at the memory, her eyes wide with fear. Marcus thanks her for sharing her story and continues his search for more information. He
Player: Piece together narrative for publication

DM:  DM:  Marcus digs deeper into the town's history, seeking out local folklore, shipwreck records, and any evidence of contact with the mysterious creatures from the deep. As he sifts through dusty archives and consults experts in various fields, he uncovers a wealth of information about the Deep Ones and their connection to Point Black Lighthouse.

After weeks of research, Marcus weaves together a comprehensive narrative that exposes the truth behind the strange occurrences at the lighthouse: the Deep Ones had returned, using the building as a gateway to the surface world. The creature witnessed by the elderly woman was just one of many summoned by the missing keeper, who had fallen under the influence of these malevolent beings.

Marcus publishes his findings in an explosive exposé, detailing the history of the Deep Ones, their connection to Point Black Lighthouse, and the sinister rituals performed by the missing keeper. The article quickly spreads across the country, sparking fear and outrage among readers.

Government officials and religious leaders are forced to acknowledge the existence of these ancient horrors, promising swift action to contain them and protect the public. Marcus' investigation not only reveals the truth behind one of the most bizarre mysteries of recent times but also serves as a warning about the dangers that lurk beneath the waves—dangers that

lighthouse_exterior.name: Point Black Lighthouse - Exterior
lighthouse_exterior.base_description: A weathered lighthouse stands on rocky black stone, its paint peeling from decades of exposure to salt spray and Atlantic winds. The structure is perhaps thirty feet tall, surrounded by a low stone wall. The keeper's cottage sits nearby, its windows dark and empty.

keeper_quarters.name: Keeper's Quarters
keeper_quarters.base_description: The keeper's sparse living space: a cot, a table with a single chair, shelves of maritime equipment. Everything is covered in dust. Through the single window, you can see the lighthouse. There's a faint smell of decay and something else—something chemical.

lighthouse_interior.name: Lighthouse Interior
lighthouse_interior.base_description: The interior of the lighthouse is a spiral of iron stairs ascending into darkness. The air is thick and stale. Each step groans under your weight. The walls are covered in moisture and a strange luminescent fungus that glows faintly green in the darkness.

keeper_room_top.name: Keeper's Room - Lighthouse Top
keeper_room_top.base_description: At the top of the lighthouse, a small room contains a cot, desk, and the great lamp mechanism. Papers are scattered everywhere—logbooks, journals, sketches of impossible symbols. The light mechanism hasn't been lit in weeks. Through the windows, you can see the entire rocky coast and far out to sea.
